CODE 2030.290(A)(1)-(2). PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-18-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-18-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-25-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2015 Continued To Feb-22-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2015 Set For Trial On Jan-19-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-03-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Having read and considered the motion, including the memorandum of points and authorities, all timely submitted admissible evidence, IT IS ORDERED THAT Plaintiff EDWARD W. SCOTT ELECTRIC CO., INC.'S Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, and For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff's request for an order compelling Defendant to provide responses to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One, is denied as moot. Plaintiff's requests for an order deeming Defendant's right to exercise the option to produce writings and to object based on privilege or work product waived is denied pursuant to Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2030.290(a)(1)-(2). Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Naomi Gray, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF EDWARD W. SCOTT ELECTRIC CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied in its entirety. Plaintiff failed to satisfy its initial burden that it is entitled to judgment as a matter of law against defendant under either res judicata or collateral estoppel. Entry of judgment on the settlement agreement against VNT does not, without more, entitle plaintiff to judgment on the settlement agreement against defendant. Plaintiff must show as a matter of law that defendant was in privity with VNT as to the motion to enforce settlement, which it did not do. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/hk
